question.text: Treasury Bills in India are issued in three tenors. RBI normally conducts weekly auctions on Wednesday for which of the following T-bill tenors?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RBI normally conducts weekly auctions (on Wednesday) for three tenors of T-bills: 91, 182, and 364 days. T-bills are zero coupon securities issued at a discount and redeemed at par, traded on the NDS-OM platform. **The Trap:** Cash Management Bills (CMBs) have maturities less than 91 days — they are not regular T-bills. Option D includes 28 days which would be a CMB, not a regular T-bill. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series IV covers T-bill pricing and yield calculations. Series I covers the relationship between T-bill rates and currency forward pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** T-bill tenors: 91/182/364 days. Auctions: Wednesday. CMBs: less than 91 days (for temporary cash flow mismatches). Both are zero-coupon, discounted instruments.
